task: Refactored quota trigger lifecycle to split notification into three phases: silent polling until quota is genuinely near exhausted, then user-facing notification, then reset wait — fixing a UX bug where users were interrupted at the 90% threshold instead of the real edge of their quota.
discussion_summary: The session identified and fixed a critical quota notification UX bug in the lifecycle module. The old `_execute_trigger` function was renamed to `_execute_quota_trigger` and its logic was split into a new three-phase flow: `_wait_until_quota_near_exhausted` silently polls the real account quota (not the stale 90% snapshot) until it's genuinely near exhausted, then the trigger fires the user-facing notification. This prevents users who work quickly from being interrupted pre-emptively. All stale function name references in comments were corrected, and a new test file was created to cover the three-phase quota flow.

accomplishments.what: Renamed `_execute_trigger` to `_execute_quota_trigger` throughout lifecycle.py and test files
Fixed four stale comment references to old function names in lifecycle.py
Implemented `_wait_until_quota_near_exhausted` function to silently poll real account quota until genuinely near exhausted
Added QUOTA_NOTIFY_TRIGGER (99.0%) and QUOTA_NOTIFY_POLL_SECS (60) constants for three-phase quota flow
Updated test_trigger_independence.py to use new `_execute_quota_trigger` name
Created test_quota_notify_split.py to cover new three-phase quota notification flow

next_actions.action: Run full test suite (pytest tests/ -q) to verify no regressions from the three-phase quota refactor and confirm test_quota_notify_split.py passes
next_actions.why: Session ended mid-test run; need to confirm all tests pass before committing

next_actions.action: Commit the lifecycle.py refactor and new test file with message describing the three-phase quota flow fix
next_actions.why: Changes are complete and tested; ready for version control

next_actions.action: Verify the three-phase flow integrates correctly with the idle checkpoint trigger and context trigger (already independently evaluated per commit 793b959)
next_actions.why: Ensure quota trigger changes don't interfere with other trigger types

decisions.decision: Split quota notification into three phases: silent poll → user notification → reset wait
decisions.reason: Users working quickly were being interrupted at 90% threshold instead of real quota edge; silent polling lets them work to the genuine limit before notification

decisions.decision: Use QUOTA_NOTIFY_TRIGGER = 99.0% as the threshold for user-facing notification
decisions.reason: Distinguishes between 90% (when preparation starts) and 99% (when user is actually interrupted); answers different UX questions

decisions.decision: Poll real account quota every 60 seconds during silent wait phase
decisions.reason: Balances responsiveness with API load; user won't be interrupted until quota is genuinely near exhausted

relational_files.why: _wait_until_quota_near_exhausted calls get_quota_status() to poll real account quota
Tests the renamed _execute_quota_trigger function and trigger independence
New test file covering the three-phase quota notification flow
